behavior_summary: When the player examines (looks at) the FIREWOOD object, the script displays a short description text 'firewood'.
Immediately after emitting the description, the script yields/suspends so control returns to the engine/player. There are no state changes, branches, menus, or global flags affected.
notable_control_flow: Entry: a U8P_FIREWOOD::look/examine event drives U8P_FIREWOOD::look (process type 0x0208).
Termination: calls suspend to yield/end the U8P_FIREWOOD::look process; no further nodes or logic follow.
open_questions: Is 'suspend' used solely to end the U8P_FIREWOOD::look process here, or could it be relied upon by other systems to resume/sequence actions? (inferred as an immediate yield; confidence: medium)
Are there other event handlers (use, touch, destroy) for this item outside this file that change its behavior? (no evidence in this script; confidence: low)
